official.name: Tim Scott
official.office_address: 104 Hart Senate Office Building Washington DC 20510

summary.narrative: This term Scott delivered fully on his FEND Off Fentanyl Act commitment (signed into law in 2024) and advanced his ROAD to Housing Act to a strong bipartisan Senate passage, though it was removed from the final NDAA. His LNG export and Empowering Main Street bills were introduced and, for LNG, advanced in the House but not enacted in the Senate, and he led organized opposition to the Basel III Endgame proposal. Several conservative-values platform items (traditional marriage, ACA mandate repeal, balanced budget amendment) show stated support but little documented legislative action this term.
summary.grade_rationale: The record pairs one fully enacted, high-magnitude law and a near-passage major housing bill with several bills that stalled at introduction or in the opposite chamber, plus a cluster of values-based platform pledges with minimal documented action. Delivery is strongest where Scott chaired the relevant committee and weakest on broad ideological commitments requiring multi-actor coalitions.

data_gaps: Many platform items (abortion-related bills c1-c3, c6-c7; Fed audit c10; spending caps c9) were sourced to issues pages and OnTheIssues without clear campaign-window attribution and lacked specific delivery evidence this term, so they were not tracked as confirmed promises. Presidential-campaign process items (RNC pledge, debate qualification) were excluded as non-policy. Several tracked promises rely on official self-reporting (year-in-review releases) which should be corroborated. Time pressure could not be computed for any promise as none stated an internal deadline.

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 57.8% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
